summary: theta-sdk-js executes DES-encrypted payload via spawned node subprocess at module load
details: theta-sdk-js is a typosquat of the Theta Labs SDK (@thetalabs/theta-js) that ships a staged dropper. src/providers/BaseProvider.js contains top-level IIFEs that, on module load, read two DES-encrypted blobs (rsa.db and des.db) from a companion runtime dependency tchain-api at node_modules/tchain-api/apps/docs/app/, decrypt them via src/decrypt.js using the hardcoded password 'hydra' (CryptoJS.DES.decrypt), and pipe the decrypted JavaScript into detached spawn('node', [], { detached: true,... }) subprocesses through stdin (rsa_exec.stdin.write(String(rsaDecrypted)) and the equivalent for the DES blob). The companion package tchain-api is declared as a runtime dependency solely to smuggle the encrypted payload bytes into the installer's node_modules — an unrelated 'API' package containing only opaque encrypted files decrypted with an in-source password is the standard split-payload dropper pattern. The payload bytes are attacker-controlled and arbitrary JavaScript executes on the installer's machine whenever code imports from theta-sdk-js/src/, and any future dist rebuild would surface the same execution channel from the main entry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
